diagrams.title: Basic DAG Structure
diagrams.description: A simple Directed Acyclic Graph with 5 vertices showing chain and branching patterns
elements: Vertex A (source, in-degree 0)
Vertex B (depends on A)
Vertex C (depends on A)
Vertex D (depends on B and C)
Vertex E (sink, out-degree 0)
Directed edges: A→B, A→C, B→D, C→D, C→E
diagrams.purpose: Understanding basic DAG structure and source/sink vertices

diagrams.title: Kahn's Algorithm Flow
diagrams.description: Step-by-step visualization of Kahn's algorithm execution
elements: Step 1: Calculate in-degrees
Step 2: Queue = {vertices with in-degree 0}
Step 3: Process vertex, reduce neighbor in-degrees
Step 4: Add new zero in-degree vertices to queue
Arrow showing reduction of in-degree values
Final result array
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the BFS-based topological sort process

diagrams.title: DFS Topological Sort
diagrams.description: DFS traversal showing finish times and stack operations
elements: DFS recursion tree with discovery and finish times
Vertices pushed to stack after exploring all neighbors
Stack showing reverse finish times
Topological order from stack pop sequence
diagrams.purpose: Understanding DFS-based approach and finish time concept

diagrams.title: Cycle Detection in Kahn's Algorithm
diagrams.description: Comparison of DAG vs cyclic graph during topological sort
elements: Left: DAG with queue processing successfully
Right: Cyclic graph with queue becoming empty
Highlighting remaining vertices with non-zero in-degree
Cycle detection message
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how cycle detection works in Kahn's algorithm
